title: Head of a White Woman Winking by Edward Taylor — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
She has one good bumblebee
which she leads about town
on a leash of clover.
It's as big as a Saint Bernard
but also extremely fragile.
People want to pet its long, shaggy coat.
These would be mostly whirling dervishes
out shopping for accessories.
meaning: "Head of a White Woman Winking" by Edward Taylor is a deep poem consisting of 19 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "She has one good bumblebee which she leads about town...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
